title: Toward a Transition Transaction Theory: Understanding the Father-Child Relationship
abstract: This article explores the evolving scholarship on the role of fathers in child development, highlighting a shift from historical neglect to contemporary focus. Despite significant advancements in theoretical frameworks, a comprehensive theory specific to the father-child relationship is lacking. Empirical evidence has demonstrated the unique contributions of fathers to child development, yet simultaneous examinations of mothers and fathers are scarce. Persistent conceptual issues, measurement challenges, and the need for inclusive models and theories underscore the importance of this research. To address these gaps, this article proposes the Transition Transaction Theory (TTT) of the father-child relationship. TTT aims to clarify fathering processes and their impact on child development over time, offering a nuanced understanding of the dynamic transitions and transactions in the father-child relationship over time. This theory has the potential to inform future research and practical applications, fostering a deeper appreciation of the father-child relationship in diverse contexts.
